title: Automating add description ID in Task Tab
excerpt:
We currently have to manually add in description identifiers(PS1,PS2 etc) into the description on the task tab, Do people know a way of using our ID column and the item ID to be able to add this automatically? 
Not sure …

posts.author: richpeers (Rich Peers)
posts.cookedHtml:
We currently have to manually add in description identifiers(PS1,PS2 etc) into the description on the task tab, Do people know a way of using our ID column and the item ID to be able to add this automatically?

Not sure if there is a formula or an App that will Help?

image1703×600 107 KB

Rich

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @richpeers

It depends where the identifier comes from (the PS1, PS2… part). Is that available in some column in the item?

posts.author: richpeers (Rich Peers)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @basdebruin no the identifier isn’t available in another column. Could we have in another board which could act as an input board?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @richpeers

I don’t think that is possible without some serious work in Make or developing an app.

posts.author: richpeers (Rich Peers)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks i was looking at make.We are building our boards with Python and Excel its having the time to pull the data in this way

Was wondering if there was a short term solution before we did this or developed an app.

posts.author: MichaelHarris (Michael Harris)
posts.cookedHtml:
@richpeers Though I have not yet used this myself, I think this tool will do what you are asking

image2922×1364 349 KB

monday.com

monday.com: Apps Marketplace

monday.com apps marketplace, Expand the capabilities of your Work OS with monday apps

Add a dropdown column on your board that has options of PS1, PS2. Then you could create an automation "If dropdown changes and contains X, Y, Z, then [Column Magic - append suffix of “X, Y, Z”]

There’s also this auto-increment column plugin that may be useful https://excellent-team.nl/solutions/autoid-column/

posts.author: richpeers (Rich Peers)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @MichaelHarris really appreciate the reply will download the app and try thanks

posts.author: richpeers (Rich Peers)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @MichaelHarris really appreciate the reply

Will download the app and try thanks

posts.author: richpeers (Rich Peers)
posts.cookedHtml: @MichaelHarris That’s amazing the column magic and a dropdown work perfectly, thanks for recommendation watched loads of @JCorrell Youtube vidoes but didn’t see column magic. Il look at the Auto ID column one aswell, saved us so much time on our implementation and soul destroying data entry.Need to figure out a form to populate it aswell somehow

posts.author: MichaelHarris (Michael Harris)
posts.cookedHtml: Yay I’m glad it worked! We are looking to implement something similar, but haven;’t gotten around to it yet
